evals.expected_output: Adds complete type annotations including parameter types, return type, and handles the Optional/None cases properly
expectations: Annotates all function parameters with types
Uses int | None for the limit parameter (modern union syntax)
Annotates the return type as list[User] or similar
Types the db parameter with a specific type or Protocol
Uses modern Python 3.10+ union syntax (| instead of Optional)

evals.prompt: I need a type-safe configuration class that requires an API key and database URL, with optional timeout and debug settings. Invalid config should fail at startup.
evals.expected_output: Creates a config class with required and optional fields, proper type annotations, and validation at construction time
expectations: Creates a dataclass or similar with typed fields
Makes api_key and database_url required (no defaults)
Makes timeout and debug optional with typed defaults
Includes validation that fails fast at construction time
Uses proper type annotations on all fields
Raises clear errors for missing required config

evals.prompt: Create a generic repository pattern with type safety so I can reuse it for User, Product, and Order entities. Each has a different ID type (str, int, UUID).
evals.expected_output: Implements a generic Repository[T, ID] base class using TypeVar and Generic that preserves type information for concrete implementations
expectations: Uses TypeVar for both the entity type and ID type
Creates a Generic base class Repository[T, ID]
Defines abstract methods with proper generic type annotations
Shows at least one concrete implementation (e.g., UserRepository)
Concrete implementation specifies exact types (Repository[User, str])
Type checker would catch mismatched types in concrete implementations
